brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +37.3% since Feb 27 — CEASEFIRE FRACTURING: Brent rebounds to ~$97-98 (Apr 9) from $94.75 Apr 8 close as Iran re-closes Hormuz following Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' in Lebanon (254 killed) — Soufan Center: truce 'on verge of collapse'
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 8 close: $94.75 (-14%). Apr 9 intraday: ~$97-98 as Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz in response to Israel's April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' strikes in Lebanon (50 fighter jets, ~160 munitions, 254 killed, 1,160+ wounded — deadliest single day of entire war). $12-18 'Hormuz risk premium' re-embedded in prices. Ceasefire technically holding on direct US-Iran hostilities (~45 hours) but fracturing on periphery. Soufan Center: 'Already Tenuous Ceasefire Hovers on the Verge of Collapse.' Islamabad Accords talks still scheduled Apr 10-11 with Vance, Witkoff, Kushner. EIA forecasts retail gas peaking $4.30/gal in April. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Soufan Center / Fortune.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +46.6% since Feb 27 — CEASEFIRE FRACTURING: WTI rebounds to ~$97-98 (Apr 9) from $94.41 Apr 8 close (-16% Apr 8, biggest drop since Apr 2020); $12-18 Hormuz risk premium re-embedded
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 8 close: $94.41 (-16%, biggest drop since April 2020). Apr 9 intraday: ~$97-98 rebounding as Iran re-closes Hormuz after Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' in Lebanon kills 254. Bloomberg Apr 9: 'Stocks dropped and oil advanced as traders watched to see if the US-Iran ceasefire holds.' Ceasefire holds on Iranian airspace but collapsing on periphery. Gas $4.166/gal nationally (AAA Apr 9) up from $4.164 yesterday. Analysts note $12-18 'Hormuz risk premium' re-embedded in prices. Windward Maritime: 'The strait has not reopened — it is in a supervised pause.' Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Windward.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.186 / +39.8% since Feb 27 — $4.166 nationally (Apr 9 AAA) — crossed $4 on Apr 2, first time since Aug 2022 — California $5.93 highest, Oklahoma $3.48 lowest
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 9: $4.166/gal nationally (AAA) — up from $4.164 yesterday and $3.478 a month ago. Gas crossed $4/gallon on April 2, first time since August 2022. Still below all-time record of $5.016 (June 2022). Diesel at $5.689/gal — up 57% year-over-year. Gasoline lags crude oil by 1-2 weeks, so the Apr 8 crude crash has not yet reached pumps — and Apr 9 rebound may limit declines. California highest at ~$5.93/gal. Oklahoma lowest at ~$3.48. Source: AAA / EIA.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -127 pts / -1.8% — CEASEFIRE REVERSAL: S&P ~6,752 Apr 9 intraday, down from Apr 8 close of 6,782.81 (+2.51% — Dow's best day since Apr 2025); VIX rebounds from 21.04 to ~23-26
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 8 close: 6,782.81 (+2.51%, Dow's best day since April 2025). Apr 9 intraday: ~6,752.64 declining as ceasefire confidence erodes. VIX: Apr 8 close 21.04 (-18.39%) → Apr 9 rising toward ~23-26. Dow Apr 8: 47,909.92 (+2.85%, +1,325 pts). Nasdaq Apr 8: 22,635.00 (+2.80%). Energy sole sector to decline Apr 8 (-3.66%). XOM -4.8% to $156.09; CVX -4.35% to $192.89. LMT -0.77% to $622.87; RTX +2.40% to $202.68. UAL +7.84% to $96.30. Gold: Apr 8 ~$4,799-4,867; Apr 9 ~$4,737-4,744. 10Y Apr 9: ~4.29%. UBS cut S&P target from 7,700 to 7,500. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / Fortune.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — CEASEFIRE HOLDING ~45 HOURS — US forces not drawn down — Hegseth/Caine: forces remain — Trump: ready for 'next conquest' — Islamabad talks Apr 10-11
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 41: ~$800M/day at reduced tempo. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~45 hours). Secretary Hegseth and CJCS Gen. Caine confirmed Apr 8 that US forces will remain in theater. Trump told CNBC Apr 9 military is ready for the 'next conquest.' ~50,000+ US troops deployed; 108 ships (78 USS, 30 USNS per USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 6). Lincoln CSG Arabian Sea ~150 miles off Oman. Bush CSG transiting, ETA Apr 10-12 providing dual-carrier capability. Tripoli ARG operational. 82nd Airborne 1st BCT (~2,000) forward-deployed. CENTCOM Apr 8: 13,000+ targets struck, 4,000 dynamic targets; 80% Iran air defenses destroyed; 150 warships sunk across 16 classes; every submarine; 97% naval mines eliminated; Iran air force zero operational flights/day. Source: Pentagon / CENTCOM / Hegseth / USNI Fleet Tracker.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (41 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$48.5B through Day 41 — CEASEFIRE FRACTURING — Trump net approval -16.3 (Silver Bulletin) — YouGov: 34% support/53% oppose war — 61% want to end war quickly — Fox News: 58% oppose
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 41: ~$48.5B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B direct costs, up to $210B total impact. Ceasefire reduces burn but forces remain fully deployed. Trump approval: CNN/SSRS 35% overall; YouGov/Economist Apr 8 37% approve/56% disapprove (net -19%); Silver Bulletin net approval -16.3 (second-term low). War polling: Pew 61% disapprove; YouGov 34% support/53% oppose (61% want war ended quickly); Fox News 58% oppose. $200B supplemental still not formally submitted to Congress. First 6 days cost $12B. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / CNN / YouGov / Silver Bulletin / Fox News.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE HOLDING ~45 HRS — 35,500+ targets cumulative — CENTCOM Apr 8: 13,000+ targets, 4,000 dynamic, 150 warships sunk across 16 classes, 80% of Iran air defenses destroyed, 97% naval mines eliminated — Iran air force: zero operational flights/day
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Apr 8 statement (Gen. Caine): 13,000+ targets struck, 4,000 dynamic targets; 80% of Iran's air defenses destroyed; 450 ballistic missile storage facilities; 800 drone storage facilities; 90% of weapons factories; 150 Iranian warships across 16 classes — every submarine sunk; 97% of naval mines eliminated. Iran's air force at zero operational flights/day (down from 30-100 pre-war). Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,500+ through Day 40. Two-week ceasefire holding on Iranian airspace ~45 hours (as of Apr 9 evening). No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7. BUT: Israel launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' against Lebanon Apr 8 — 50 fighter jets delivering ~160 munitions against 100+ targets in 10 minutes — single largest coordinated strike of entire war — 254 killed, 1,160+ wounded. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13-15 US KIA — CENTCOM Apr 8: 13 KIA / 348-381 WIA (Military Times: 346, CENTCOM: 381) — Intercept: 520+ WIA — 15 Americans wounded Ali Al Salem Kuwait Apr 7 — F-15E/A-10 crews fully recovered — ceasefire holding
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13-15 KIA confirmed: 6 soldiers in Port Shuaiba Kuwait drone strike (Mar 1), 1 in Saudi Arabia, 6 airmen in KC-135 refueling tanker crash over Iraq (Mar 12). Manifest CENTCOM figure: 13 KIA / 348-381 WIA (Military Times 346, CENTCOM 381). The Intercept April 8: Pentagon may be undercounting wounded (520+). 15 Americans wounded by Iranian drone strike at Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait on April 7 — last major attack before ceasefire. F-15E crew shot down April 2 fully rescued April 4 in separate CSAR missions. A-10 pilot also recovered. Total aircraft losses: 7 airframes + 12+ MQ-9 Reaper drones. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~45 hours). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: CENTCOM: 381 WIA / Military Times: 346 / Intercept: 520+ — 15 wounded Ali Al Salem Kuwait Apr 7 — Pentagon may be undercounting (Intercept Apr 8) — ceasefire holding ~45 hrs
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. Sources vary: CENTCOM cites 381 wounded; Military Times cites 346; The Intercept reports 520+, with April 8 report accusing Pentagon of undercounting. 15 Americans wounded by Iranian drone strike at Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait on April 7 — the last major attack before ceasefire. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 — no confirmed new WIA April 8-9. Estimated 535+ total WIA using higher figures. Source: CENTCOM / Military Times / The Intercept / Pentagon.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ / HRANA Apr 7: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military) / Hengaw Apr 2: 7,300+ (6,410 military) — 195 cities / 27 provinces — 334 health facilities damaged — 3.2M displaced — 68,000+ Iranians fled to Turkey
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting Iran's internet blackout (at 4% of normal) and government underreporting. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded (216 children, 251 women as of Apr 3). HRANA (Apr 7): 3,636 killed (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military). Hengaw 9th Report (Apr 2): 7,300+ (890 civilians, 6,410 military, 180 minors) — documented strikes across 195 cities in 27 provinces. Hengaw's military figure unrecognized by Iran's government. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. 334 health/emergency centers damaged (IRCS). 763 schools damaged. 115,193 civilian structures damaged. 3.2M IDPs; 68,000+ fled to Turkey; 70,000+ Afghans returned. Ceasefire holding on Iranian territory ~45 hrs. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / WHO / OCHA / UNHCR.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,530-1,754+ killed incl. 254 in ONE DAY from Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' Apr 8 — 50 fighter jets, 160 munitions, 100+ targets in 10 MIN — 1,160+ wounded — Netanyahu: ceasefire EXCLUDES Lebanon — Lebanon declared Apr 9 day of mourning
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli ground invasion since March 2. 1,530-1,754+ killed including 125+ children. DEADLIEST DAY: April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — Israel launched 50 fighter jets delivering ~160 munitions against 100+ targets across Lebanon in 10 minutes — the single largest coordinated strike of the entire war. 254 killed, 1,160+ wounded across central Beirut, southern Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, Sidon, and Tyre. Central Beirut neighborhoods hit without prior warning. Lebanon declared April 9 a national day of mourning. UN Human Rights Chief Volker Turk: 'nothing short of horrific.' Netanyahu April 8 press conference: 'The two-week ceasefire does not include Lebanon... We still have some goals to accomplish.' White House confirmed via Press Secretary Leavitt. European leaders (Germany, France, Italy, UK, Denmark, Spain, Netherlands, Canada) + EU issued joint statement demanding Lebanon's inclusion. Iran's position: Lebanon IS included — IRGC re-closed Hormuz in response, citing ceasefire violation. Hezbollah launched retaliatory missiles early Apr 9. 1M+ displaced (700,000+ in crowded shelters). Three IDF divisions south of Litani. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UN Human Rights / UNICEF / UNHCR / Macron / EU.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: UNHCR: 7.5M+ — Iran 3.2M IDPs — Lebanon 1M+ (700K in shelters) — 68,000+ Iranians fled to Turkey — 70,000+ Afghans returned from Iran — UNHCR Lebanon only 14% funded against $472M requirement
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR estimates 3.2 million internally displaced Iranians, fleeing urban centers to rural/northern areas. Lebanon: over 1 million displaced, 700,000+ in crowded shelters. 150,000 cut off south of Litani. 68,000+ Iranians arrived in Turkey. 70,000+ Afghan refugees returned to Afghanistan from Iran in first two weeks of March (Iran hosts 4.4M Afghans). Ceasefire holding in Iran may slow Iranian displacement but Lebanon explicitly excluded per Netanyahu. UNHCR Lebanon response only 14% funded against $472M requirement. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — Hormuz RE-CLOSED by Iran Apr 8 after Eternal Darkness — Windward: 'supervised pause, not reopened' — ~800 tankers stuck in Persian Gulf — 3,200 total vessels, 20,000 seafarers
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz status: The IRGC announced it was RE-CLOSING the Strait of Hormuz on April 8, accusing Israel of violating ceasefire after 'Operation Eternal Darkness' in Lebanon. White House (Leavitt Apr 8) claims 'Strait is open'; Bloomberg, CNBC, Windward Maritime contradict — 'The strait has not reopened — it is in a supervised pause.' Apr 7: 11 vessels transited via IRGC-controlled Northern Corridor near Larak. Apr 8: only 5 bulk carriers outbound by noon — no major oil tankers. Approximately 800 tankers and cargo ships (~3,200 total vessels, ~20,000 seafarers) remain stuck inside Persian Gulf (Bloomberg). War-risk insurance remains unavailable. Iran attacked 29 commercial ships (13 tankers), killing 11 seafarers. Iran and Oman reportedly plan to charge transit fees earmarked for reconstruction. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / CNBC / Windward / Fars News.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA Iran: 244+ — Lebanon: 125+ (UNICEF) — Iran Health Min: 216 children (Apr 3) — 3 boys injured in post-ceasefire cluster submunitions Beersheba — 763 schools damaged — Eternal Darkness Apr 8 casualties include children (unverified breakdown)
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran. Iran Health Ministry: 216 children killed (as of Apr 3). UNICEF: 125+ children killed in Lebanon. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7. Post-ceasefire violations included cluster submunitions hitting Beersheba Apr 7, lightly injuring 3 boys. April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' in Lebanon (254 total killed) certainly included child casualties but no specific breakdown published. Deadliest single incident: Minab girls' school (Feb 28) — 165-180 killed. WHO verified 23 attacks on healthcare causing 9 deaths. 763 schools damaged. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / Amnesty International.
